data.title: Infini Hacker Wallet Converts Ethereum to Wrapped Bitcoin
data.description: Onchain Lens posted on X that a wallet, potentially associated with the Infini Hacker, has exchanged 3,003 Ethereum for 87 Wrapped Bitcoin. The transaction, valued at $5.89 million, was executed at a price of $67,700 per WBTC. Following the swap, the assets were transferred to another wallet. This activity highlights ongoing movements within the cryptocurrency space, drawing attention to the actions of entities linked to previous security breaches.

data.title: Bitdeer Reports Stable Bitcoin Holdings Amid Mining and Sales
data.description: Bitdeer, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company, has released its latest Bitcoin holdings data on the X platform. According to PANews, the company produced 189.8 BTC through mining this week, while selling an equivalent amount of 189.8 BTC. As a result, Bitdeer's net Bitcoin holdings did not increase, maintaining a total of approximately 943.1 BTC as of February 20.

data.title: Georgia Town Faces Backlash Over Migrant Internment Camp Plans
data.description: A new internment camp for migrants is planned to open in Social Circle, Georgia, sparking opposition from local residents. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the resistance from Trump voters in the area. The community is concerned about the impact on their town and the implications of such a facility. The debate reflects broader national tensions surrounding immigration policies and their local effects.

data.title: Bitcoin Approaches Bear Market Bottom as Conviction Buyers Accumulate
data.description: On-chain analysis suggests that Bitcoin is approaching the bottom of the bear market. According to NS3.AI, current data reveals patterns similar to the bottoming process observed in June 2022. Conviction Buyers have amassed a record 3.48 million BTC during this cycle, exceeding the accumulation levels seen during past market crises, including the 5.19, LUNA, and FTX events. Historically, the continuous accumulation by this group indicates a supply-demand balance that aligns with the formation of bear market bottoms.

data.title: Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena Prepares for New Board of Directors
data.description: Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena has approved a preliminary list of candidates for its upcoming board of directors, according to individuals familiar with the situation. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the bank's strategic move as it seeks to strengthen its governance structure. The decision comes amid ongoing efforts to stabilize the institution and enhance its operational efficiency. The bank, one of Italy's oldest financial institutions, is navigating through a challenging economic environment and aims to bolster its leadership team to address current and future challenges effectively. The selection process for the new board members is expected to be thorough, ensuring that the candidates align with the bank's strategic goals and regulatory requirements. This development marks a significant step in the bank's ongoing transformation efforts.

data.title: Korean Culture and Global Storytelling Blend in New Animation
data.description: KPop Demon Hunters, an animated film by Korean-Canadian animator Maggie Kang, explores the fusion of Korean culture with global narratives. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Kang's passion for Korean pop music, which she embraced long before it gained international popularity. The director discusses the film's themes and the unifying power of music in a recent interview.

data.title: IoTeX confirms ‘suspicious activity’ involving token safe, says losses contained
data.description:
IoTeX said it is assessing suspicious activity tied to a token safe, coordinating with exchanges to trace funds after analysts linked the incident to a possible private key compromise.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/iotex-investigates-token-safe-suspicious-activity-4-3m-loss?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: CME Group Expands Crypto Trading to 24/7, Aiming to Reduce Bitcoin Volatility
data.description: CME Group has announced the expansion of its crypto derivatives trading to a 24/7 schedule, eliminating previous restrictions on weekend trading hours. According to NS3.AI, analysts suggest that this development will enhance liquidity and mitigate Bitcoin's weekend price fluctuations. The change is anticipated to offer traders more consistent access and facilitate smoother market dynamics throughout the week.

data.title: Potential Impact of Clarity Act on Bitcoin Market
data.description:
The probability of the Clarity Act passing recently surged to 90% before settling at around 70%, according to ChainCatcher. This legislation is seen as a potential key factor in the next phase of Bitcoin's market movement, with many considering it a significant trigger for a Bitcoin rebound, similar to how the 'Genius Act' previously boosted stablecoin growth.

Dennis Porter, co-founder of the Satoshi Action Fund, stated that once the Clarity Act is signed, it could open the floodgates for trillions of dollars in capital waiting for a regulatory framework to enter the market. The general consensus in the market is that substantial progress on the Clarity Act could serve as a crucial catalyst for a new influx of funds into the crypto market.

data.title: Bitcoin prices stall – Why BTC’s recovery isn’t confirmed yet
data.description:
The monthly cumulative Bitcoin demand has turned positive after nearly three consecutive months of low demand.
source: https://ambcrypto.com/bitcoin-prices-stuck-why-btcs-recovery-isnt-confirmed-yet/

data.title: Japan's Economic Growth Discussed by Apollo CEO
data.description: Japan's economic resurgence is drawing attention from global investors, as highlighted by the CEO of Apollo, a leading alternative investment firm. Bloomberg posted on X, emphasizing the significance of Japan's return to growth. The CEO discussed the factors contributing to this economic revival and the potential opportunities it presents for investors. The conversation sheds light on Japan's evolving economic landscape and its implications for the global market.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | ECB President Christine Lagarde Leaves WEF Speech Over Anti-European Remarks
data.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde reportedly exited a speech by U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick at the World Economic Forum due to his anti-European rhetoric. Bloomberg posted on X that Lagarde found Lutnick's comments inappropriate for the event's context. The incident highlights ongoing tensions between European and American officials over economic and political matters. Lagarde's decision to leave underscores the sensitivity surrounding international discourse at high-profile gatherings like the WEF.

data.title: Bitcoin sees inflows as ETH from Bybit hack via THORChain
data.description:
Flow data shows stolen ETH was swapped to BTC via THORChain after the breach; routes remain traceable — Bybit hack, THORChain laundering, ETH to BTC swaps.
source: https://coincu.com/news/bitcoin-sees-inflows-as-eth-from-bybit-hack-via-thorchain/

data.title: China Tightens Regulations on Overseas Real World Assets
data.description: The recent issuance of Document No. 42 has highlighted China's stringent regulatory oversight on overseas Real World Assets (RWA) connected to the country, especially those involving domestic securities or funds. According to NS3.AI, despite the increased regulations, CICC Hong Kong has begun engaging with several public blockchains and exchanges to explore potential business collaborations. Major institutions such as Ant Group and JD.com are also closely monitoring the changing regulatory landscape.

data.title: Caixin: Document No. 42 emphasizes strict regulation of overseas RWA; CICC Hong Kong has contacted public blockchains and exchanges.
data.description: Over the weekend of the release of the "Notice on Further Preventing and Handling Risks Related to Virtual Assets" (Document No. 42), the CICC Hong Kong team contacted several public blockchains and exchanges to explore potential business cooperation. Another public blockchain executive stated their desire to explore cooperation opportunities with investment banks and other intermediaries, while Ant Group and JD.com also expressed concern about the policy changes. The report noted that Hong Kong is one of the overseas issuance locations for RWAs. Sources familiar with the regulations stated that RWAs with Hong Kong assets as underlying assets are not within the scope of Document No. 42; however, if domestic securities or funds are used as underlying assets and RWAs are issued overseas, the relevant departments of the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) are responsible. The relevant statements emphasize that strict regulation will be implemented for RWAs issued overseas using domestic assets, and this should not be interpreted as an encouragement or a signal of relaxed regulation. (Caixin.com)

data.title: Quantum Computing Is Not Imminent Threat to Bitcoin: Bitfinex
data.description:
Bitfinex has confirmed that quantum computing is not yet sophisticated enough to threaten Bitcoin.
source: https://u.today/quantum-computing-is-not-imminent-threat-to-bitcoin-bitfinex

data.title: BNP Paribas Issues Tokenized Money Market Fund on Ethereum
data.description:
BNP Paribas Asset Management has successfully issued a tokenized share class of a French-domiciled money market fund (MMF) on the public Ethereum blockchain. The pilot marks a shift from the bank’s previous tokenization experiments, which were conducted on private blockchain infrastructure in Luxembourg.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/bnp-paribas-issues-tokenized-money-market-fund-on-ethereum/
